title: Module 1
content:
Introduction
to
bioremediation,
Historical
development
of
environmental bioremediation, Requirements for bioremediation,
Constraints and priorities of bioremediation, Factors affecting
bioremediation
Bioremediation; Societal and legal  background
Biodegradation- principles and microbiology; Limitations of
bioremediation
Overall view on in situ and ex-situ bioremediation techniques.
Advantages  and disadvantages

title: Module 2
content:
Bioremediation strategies – biostimulation and bioaugmentation,
Biosorption, and precipitation.
Biodegradation kinetics, Bioavailability, Biomineralization, Testing
for biodegradability
Microbes for bioremediation: Essential characteristics of microbes
for Bioremediation - Microbial adaptation for adverse conditions -
Metabolic process involved in bioremediation.
Advantages and disadvantages of specific bioremediation
technologies - Land farming - Prepared beds - Biopiles, composting,
constructed wetlands,

title: Module 3
content:
Bioremediation of Soil and  water contaminated with oil and
pesticides- Effects of Cosubstrates on microorganisms – Rhizo
remediation-  applications’
Gaseous bioremediation, bio-scrubbers, bioventing, Soil Vapour
Extraction (SVE), Water recirculation systems, Air sparging,
Biobarriers,
Bioremediation technologies for heavy metal and radionuclides
removal: Microbial transformation - Accumulation and concentration
of metal, Microbial interactions with heavy metals - resistance
&tolerance -Biosorption of heavy metals
Biosurfactants in bioremediation

title: Module 4
content:
Emerging Techniques:  Role of biosensors in bioremediation
Nanomaterials for bioremediation of air pollution
Phytoremediation and its processes, role of phytochelatins.
Applications of genetic engineering in phytoremediation.
Algal and fungal-based bioremediation.,
Biomonitoring - Application of Microbial Enzymes - Construction of
Biomembrane Reactors for Bioremediation
Biodegradation of polyhalogenated compounds by genetically
engineered bacteria.
